vectors.name: Length Function
vectors.description: Returns the dynamic length of a string.

quirks_and_tests.description: Standard length evaluation. Note that in memory, this dynamically reads the byte value at index 0 of the string array.

vectors.name: Copy Function
vectors.syntax: function Copy(S: String; Index: Integer; Count: Integer): String;
vectors.description: Returns a substring containing Count characters, starting from Index.

quirks_and_tests.description: If the Index is greater than the string length, Copy returns an empty string. If Count exceeds the remaining characters, it gracefully returns only what is available without throwing out-of-bounds errors.

vectors.name: Pos Function
vectors.syntax: function Pos(Substr: String; S: String): Byte;
vectors.description: Searches for a substring and returns the 1-based index of its first occurrence.

quirks_and_tests.description: The arguments are ordered as Substring first, Target second. It is strictly case-sensitive. Returns 0 if not found.

vectors.name: Delete Procedure
vectors.syntax: procedure Delete(var S: String; Index: Integer; Count: Integer);
vectors.description: Removes Count characters from string S, starting at Index.

quirks_and_tests.description: CRITICAL QUIRK: This is a Procedure, not a Function. It mutates the string variable IN PLACE. If Index is out of bounds, it simply does nothing.

vectors.name: Insert Procedure
vectors.syntax: procedure Insert(Source: String; var S: String; Index: Integer);
vectors.description: Inserts a Source string into target string S at the specified Index.

quirks_and_tests.description: Like Delete, it mutates the target string IN PLACE. If Index is greater than the current length of S, the Source string is simply concatenated at the end.

vectors.name: Str Procedure (Number to String)
vectors.syntax: procedure Str(X [: Width [: Decimals]]; var S: String);
vectors.description: Converts a numeric value to its string representation.

quirks_and_tests.description: This is a procedure that populates a target variable. Crucially, it does not prepend a leading space for positive numbers. It supports standard WriteLn-style formatting constraints.

vectors.name: Val Procedure (String to Number)
vectors.syntax: procedure Val(S: String; var V; var Code: Integer);
vectors.description: Converts a string to its numeric value. The target variable V is untyped (can be Integer, Longint, or Real), but the error Code variable MUST be strictly a 16-bit Integer.

quirks_and_tests.description: CRITICAL COMPILE-TIME QUIRK: Turbo Pascal enforces strict var-parameter typing. Passing a Longint for the 'Code' parameter will throw 'Error 89: Type mismatch' at compile time. RUNTIME QUIRK: If parsing fails, the target numeric variable remains unchanged, and Code is set to the 1-based index of the invalid character.

vectors.name: Direct Index Access & Length Byte
vectors.description: Characters in a string can be accessed directly as an array. Index 0 holds the length.

quirks_and_tests.description: CRITICAL QUIRK: In Turbo Pascal, a String is an array of characters from 0 to 255. Index 0 stores the dynamic length of the string as a character byte. Reading the Ord() of index 0 is functionally equivalent to calling Length().
